{

    "business_context": "A global logistics platform adding real-time earnings transparency for drivers across three jurisdictions with fundamentally different labor classifications, legal requirements, and institutional approval processes.       { "term": "Base Pay", "definition": "The fixed compensation component for a work period or shift, independent of delivery volume. For employees (CA), this must meet or exceed minimum wage. For contractors (Indonesia), this is a per-engagement rate." },
      { "term": "Per-Delivery Bonus", "definition": "Variable compensation tied to each completed delivery. Calculation rules differ by jurisdiction and worker classification." },
      { "term": "Tips", "definition": "Gratuities from customers passed through to the driver. In California, tips must never be counted toward minimum wage obligations and must be shown as a separate line item." },
      { "term": "Deductions", "definition": "Amounts subtracted from gross earnings: equipment fees, platform fees, tax withholdings, insurance, etc. In California, every deduction must be itemized with a legal basis.       { "term": "Earnings Breakdown", "definition": "The structured view of all compensation components for a given period, presented to the driver." },
      { "term": "Worker Classification", "definition": "The legal status of the driver — employee or independent contractor — which determines which wage, tax, and transparency laws apply. This is a jurisdiction-level attribute, not a driver preference." },
      { "term": "Works Council (Betriebsrat)", "definition": "A German statutory employee representative body with co-determination rights. Changes to compensation display systems require formal works council approval before deployment under the BetrVG (Works Constitution Act)." },
      { "term": "Deduction Authorization", "definition": "In California, a deduction from employee wages requires prior written authorization from the employee, with limited exceptions for legally mandated withholdings." },
      { "term": "Real-Time", "definition": "Earnings data updated and visible to the driver within the current pay period, reflecting completed deliveries. Does NOT mean sub-second latency — means current-period accuracy, not end-of-period batch." }
    ]
  },

    {

      "invariant_justification": "The feature's core promise is transparency. Merging or hiding components defeats the purpose and violates California's itemized wage statement requirement (Labor Code §226). A corrupt breakdown is worse than no breakdown.",

      "invariant_justification": "Under BetrVG §87(1) No. 6, the works council has co-determination rights over technical systems that monitor employee performance or behavior. The system is in a corrupt state if it shows compensation data to German drivers without recorded approval.",

      "invariant_justification": "This is a business policy decision, not a legal mandate. The platform could choose to apply California-level transparency globally as a competitive differentiator, or could keep Indonesia minimal. A VP-level decision.",
